intro: Tithymalus irgisensis, also known as the Irgis spurge, is a plant species belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family, characterized by its distinctive milky latex. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, offering potential health benefits in the treatment of various conditions such as arthritis and gastrointestinal disorders. Bioactive constituents present in Tithymalus irgisensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Tithymalus irgisensis is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being used in the form of a tincture or salve, to harness its therapeutic benefits.
